title: Insurers pivot AI strategy toward core risk underwriting

raw_body:
AI investments by insurers are now expected to generate tangible business value beyond mere efficiency. According to findings in the 2026 Evident AI Index, insurers are now embedding AI technologies into workflows that directly influence underwriting discipline and capital allocation. Christian Preece, Insurance Director at Evident, says: “For years, insurers have competed on AI ambition, […]
The post Insurers pivot AI strategy toward core risk underwriting appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Insurers are shifting their AI strategies to focus on core risk underwriting, moving beyond efficiency to create real business value. The 2026 Evident AI Index highlights this trend, showing that AI is now integrated into workflows that affect underwriting and capital allocation. Christian Preece from Evident notes a shift from merely competing on AI ambition to practical applications. This matters now as insurers look to enhance decision-making and profitability in a competitive market.
why_it_matters: Insurers can expect improved underwriting accuracy, leading to better risk assessment and pricing for policyholders.
This shift reflects a broader trend in the insurance industry towards leveraging technology for strategic advantage, potentially reshaping market dynamics.

lenses.eli12: Insurers are starting to use AI not just to save time but to make smarter decisions about risk. Think of it like using a GPS for driving—it's not just about getting there faster, but finding the best route. This shift could mean better insurance options and prices for everyone.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this change indicates a growing user need for tools that enhance underwriting precision. Investing in AI that delivers actionable insights can lead to cost savings and improved efficiency. Companies could focus on developing solutions that integrate seamlessly into existing workflows.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the integration of AI into underwriting processes suggests a focus on predictive models that enhance risk evaluation. Insurers are likely utilizing advanced machine learning techniques to analyze data more effectively, which could lead to more accurate capital allocation. This transition emphasizes the importance of robust data pipelines and model validation in production.
